 {"id":"bd-1dt84","title":"Feature: Budget & Resource Control - Token caps and early stopping","description":"# Feature: Budget & Resource Control

**Critical for preventing token black holes.** Ensemble runs can be expensive - this feature provides predictability and control.

## Budget Configuration

### BudgetConfig (consistent with types.go)
```yaml
budget:
  per_agent: 4000      # Max tokens per mode agent
  total: 50000         # Max for entire ensemble run
  synthesis: 8000      # Reserved for synthesis phase
  context_pack: 2000   # Reserved for context injection
```

### Early Stopping (Marginal Utility)
Stop when new findings rate drops below threshold:
```go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if new findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
}
```

### Context Pack Caching
- Cache generated context packs
- Reuse across ensemble runs
- Invalidate on code changes or explicit request
- Track cache hit rate in metrics

## Real-Time Monitoring

### Token Velocity Dashboard
```
Budget: 23,456 / 50,000 tokens (47%)
████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░

Agent Spend:
[0] F1 Argument Mapping    3,847 tokens  ✓
[1] E2 Root Cause          4,012 tokens  ✓ (cap hit)
[2] K1 Systems Thinking    2,891 tokens  ◐
[3] L3 Meta-cognitive      pending
[4] H2 Strategic           pending

Findings Rate: 2.3 findings/1k tokens
Early Stop: disabled (4 more agents needed)
```

## Alerts & Guardrails
- Warn at 80% budget consumed
- Auto-stop at 100% with partial synthesis
- Log all cap hits for tuning
- Suggest budget adjustments based on history

## Files
- internal/ensemble/budget.go - Budget types and tracking
- internal/ensemble/early_stop.go - Marginal utility detection
- internal/ensemble/metrics.go - Findings velocity, similarity
- internal/tui/budget_panel.go - Dashboard component","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.239196966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586537664Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586517856Z","close_reason":"Core Budget & Resource Control implemented: BudgetTracker (budget.go) with per-agent/total tracking and enforcement, EarlyStopDetector (early_stop.go) with findings rate and similarity thresholds, token estimation via internal/tokens, ContextPackCache (context_cache.go) with TTL and invalidation. All components have comprehensive unit tests and integration tests. EarlyStop is wired into pipeline stage2. TUI dashboard (metrics_panel.go) and detailed metrics are scoped to bd-397f0 (Observability Metrics).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ed7","title":"E2E Tests: Full session recovery workflow","description":"## End-to-End Test Suite for Session Recovery

### E2E Test Scenarios
1. **Clean session save/restore**
   - Spawn session with 3 agents
   - Execute some commands
   - Kill session (simulate crash)
   - Run ntm recover
   - Verify all panes restored with scrollback

2. **Partial recovery**
   - Spawn session
   - Corrupt one pane's state file
   - Recover
   - Verify remaining panes restored
   - Verify error reported for corrupted pane

3. **Recovery after long idle**
   - Spawn session
   - Wait 30+ seconds
   - Kill and recover
   - Verify state still valid

4. **Multi-project recovery**
   - Spawn sessions for 2 projects
   - Kill both
   - Recover each independently

### Detailed Logging
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Spawning session: %d panes", numPanes)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] State captured: %d bytes", stateSize)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Simulating crash...")
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Restoring pane %d/%d: %s", i, total, paneName)
log.Printf("[E2E-RECOVERY] Scrollback match: %v (%d lines)", match, lines)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T06:12:50.783368496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:34:19.380086093Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:34:19.380006103Z","close_reason":"E2E test suite implemented with 5 comprehensive tests: TestFullSessionRecovery (full spawn/crash/recover cycle), TestPartialRecovery (corruption handling), TestRecoveryAfterLongIdle (idle period resilience), TestMultiProjectRecovery (cross-project recovery), TestRecoveryContextBuilding (context validation). All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ed7","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ed7","depends_on_id":"bd-3law","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1edma","title":"Task: Implement ntm --robot-jfp-* wrapper","description":"## Overview
Implement robot wrapper commands that proxy to `jfp` with JSON output.

## Responsibilities
- Call `jfp <cmd> --json` (or rely on non-TTY JSON) and validate schema
- Normalize output into NTM robot envelope when appropriate
- Map jfp exit codes to NTM error codes (NOT_FOUND, INVALID_FLAG, DEPENDENCY_MISSING)
- Support `--project` installs when requested

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each wrapper returns stable JSON
- Errors include actionable hints (install jfp, invalid id, etc.)
- No prompts in robot mode","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

All commands return stable JSON with proper error codes (DEPENDENCY_MISSING, NOT_FOUND, INVALID_FLAG) and actionable hints.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1edma","depends_on_id":"bd-1q6xh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1edma","depends_on_id":"bd-3kfv0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1es69","title":"process_triage: Implement background health monitoring loop","description":"Create background goroutine that monitors agent health via pt.

## Requirements

1. Monitor all agent panes continuously
2. Call pt with agent PIDs at configured interval
3. Track classification history
4. Trigger alerts on threshold breach

## Implementation

```go
package pt

type HealthMonitor struct {
    config      *config.ProcessTriageConfig
    pidMap      *rano.PIDMap  // reuse from rano integration
    states      map[string]*AgentState  // pane -> state
    alertCh     chan Alert
    stopCh      chan struct{}
}

type AgentState struct {
    Pane           string
    Classification string  // useful, waiting, idle, stuck, zombie
    Since          time.Time
    History        []ClassificationEvent
}

func (m *HealthMonitor) Start() {
    go m.monitorLoop()
}

func (m *HealthMonitor) monitorLoop() {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(time.Duration(m.config.CheckInterval) * time.Second)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            m.checkAll()
        case <-m.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}

func (m *HealthMonitor) checkAll() {
    for pane, pid := range m.pidMap.GetAll() {
        classification := m.classify(pid)
        m.updateState(pane, classification)
        m.checkAlerts(pane)
    }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/integrations/pt/monitor.go

## Integration with rano
If rano enabled, include network activity in classification:
- Network activity → likely waiting, not stuck","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:21:56.937143241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T01:42:55.094897305Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T01:42:55.094809931Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-1h5xe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-1sa5u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-27l8q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1es69","depends_on_id":"bd-lzvh2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}
